author: Reviewer on bad apps.
title: NEEDS A MAJOR REDESIGN!!!
review:
Telebirr has potential, but the experience isn’t smooth. The app feels glitchy, clunky, and often lags when you need it most. Simple actions like sending or receiving money sometimes take longer than they should, and the interface can feel confusing.                                                    
What Telebirr really needs is a cleaner, simpler design something that works great and is easy for everyone, young or old, to use without frustration. If the app focused more on speed, stability, and user-friendliness while including minimality but not over doing it,  it could truly become the go-to mobile wallet in Ethiopia.

author: Sofoniasdes
title: Worst App Ever
review: I want to express my deep dissatisfaction with this mobile money payment application. The app is riddled with issues such as frequent crashes, an unresponsive customer service hotline, and an arduous user-level upgrade process. Its limitations on fund transfers and its reputation as the worst-rated mobile money app in Africa underscore the urgent need for improvement. Addressing these concerns promptly would greatly enhance user experience, credibility, and the app's overall utility. I suggest the developers should go to Kenya or Nigeria or South Africa to see how flawlessly such apps function and improve the livelihood of the society in general.

author: Ewnetu Ze Nefas Silk
title: Not Seniors friendly
review:
The idea for the app was very good. Unfortunately, it is excluding a section of the Ethio Telecom's customers, namely seniors. The date selector of the app is implemented in a bad way. If you are born beyond 1960, it will not allow you to register. The date of birth field is a mandatory field. You cannot leave it blank. So, if like me you would like to test the app, you will be forced to give a false date of birth. Why make a field mandatory when it cannot accept all possible values. This is a bad app design. 

Second, I had entered my name with the correct spelling. But, I see it replaced with a wrong spelling and the app does not give a means not correcting wrong entries. Since I am a customer of Ethio Telecom, the replacement of my correctly spelled name by some thing wrong tells me my name exists with a wrong spelling on Ethio Telecom's directory. 

I cannot tell about other facts of the app yet since I am refraining to use it until I can enter my correct name and date of birth.
